entries.definition:
كتاب الجيم

جوز

(جوع) الجيم والواو والعين، كلمةٌ واحدة. فالجوع ضِدّ الشّبَع. ويقال: عام مَجاعةٍ ومَجوعة .

j–w–ʿ hunger, to hunger, to yearn for something, to starve, starvation, famine. Of this root, two forms occur five times in the Qurʾan: تَجُوع tajūʿ once and جُوع jūʿ four times.
تَجُوع tajūʿ [imperf. of v. جَاعَ jāʿa, intrans.] to become or go hungry, to hunger (20:118) إِنَّ لَكَ أَلَّا تَجُوعَ فِيهَا وَلَا تَعْرَىٰ it is [granted] for you that in it [the Garden] you will not go hungry, or naked.

جُوع jūʿ [n./v. n.] hunger, starvation (106:4) ٱلَّذِىٓ أَطْعَمَهُم مِّن جُوعٍ He who has removed hunger from them [lit. fed them away from hunger], and made them safe [away] from fear.
